notes: Civil liberties item: allow homosexual to teach at college. Dropped from 2022/2024 GSS; not in 2024 codebook. Companion items COLATH, COLRAC, COLCOM, COLMSLM are in the analytic core.
Civil liberties item: allow militarist to teach at college. Dropped from 2022/2024 GSS; not in 2024 codebook. Companion items COLATH, COLRAC, COLCOM, COLMSLM are in the analytic core.
Hours per week on email — continuous item, not binary-recodable. Last fielded 2018; not in 2022/2024.
Minutes per week on email — continuous item, not binary-recodable. Last fielded 2018; not in 2022/2024.

5-category immigration attitude item (Asian immigrants). Recoded in 01_data_preparation.qmd but absent from GLM and forest outputs. Fielded only once pre-2022 (single entry in vars-by-year); not in 2016/2018 baseline.

5-category immigration attitude item (Latin American immigrants). Recoded in 01_data_preparation.qmd but absent from GLM and forest outputs. Fielded only once pre-2022 (single entry in vars-by-year); not in 2016/2018 baseline.
Civil liberties item: allow homosexual's book in public library. Dropped from 2022/2024 GSS; not in 2024 codebook. Companion items LIBATH, LIBRAC, LIBCOM, LIBMSLM are in the analytic core.
Civil liberties item: allow militarist's book in public library. Dropped from 2022/2024 GSS; not in 2024 codebook. Companion items LIBATH, LIBRAC, LIBCOM, LIBMSLM are in the analytic core.
Racial contact item: favor/oppose close relative living in neighborhood with Black people. Fielded in 2022 but not 2024 (NOT AVAILABLE IN THIS RELEASE in 2024 codebook).
Racial contact item: favor/oppose close relative living in neighborhood with White people. Confirmed not fielded in 2022/2024.
Not fielded in 2024 (coded NOT AVAILABLE IN THIS RELEASE). 5-point favor/oppose scale re close relative marrying an Asian American person. Fielded in 2022; would require 2022-only analysis.
Not fielded in 2024 (coded NOT AVAILABLE IN THIS RELEASE). 5-point favor/oppose scale re close relative marrying a Black person. Fielded in 2022; would require 2022-only analysis.
Not fielded in 2024 (coded NOT AVAILABLE IN THIS RELEASE). 5-point favor/oppose scale re close relative marrying a Hispanic/Latino person. Fielded in 2022; would require 2022-only analysis.
Not fielded in 2024 (coded NOT AVAILABLE IN THIS RELEASE). 5-point favor/oppose scale re close relative marrying a White person. Fielded in 2022; would require 2022-only analysis.
National spending item (mass transportation). Not fielded in 2022/2024. Companion natspend items are in the analytic core.
National spending item (parks and recreation). Not fielded in 2022/2024. Companion natspend items are in the analytic core.

Not fielded in 2016/2018 — new item introduced in 2024. Asks whether racial differences are due to discrimination (1=Yes, 2=No). Note: coding direction is opposite to RACDIF1-4 where 1=conservative response; if added to future analyses, recode as to0=1, to1=2 for directional consistency.
Civil liberties item: allow homosexual to speak in community. Dropped from 2022/2024 GSS; not in 2024 codebook. Companion items SPKATH, SPKRAC, SPKCOM, SPKMSLM are in the analytic core.
Civil liberties item: allow militarist to speak in community. Dropped from 2022/2024 GSS; not in 2024 codebook. Companion items SPKATH, SPKRAC, SPKCOM, SPKMSLM are in the analytic core.
Not fielded in 2024 (confirmed via 2024 codebook distribution tables). SUICIDE1, SUICIDE2, SUICIDE4 are in the analytic core. Would require 2022-only analysis.
Hours per week on internet — continuous item, not binary-recodable as attitude measure. Last fielded 2018; not in 2022/2024.
Minutes per week on internet (companion to WWWHR) — continuous, not binary-recodable. Last fielded 2018; not in 2022/2024.
Pre-2022 name for MARSAME. Renamed in 2022 GSS; wording otherwise identical. See MARSAME notes for harmonization details.

Rename of MARHOMO (pre-2022). Scale: 1=Strongly Agree to 5=Strongly Disagree. ~65% NOT APPLICABLE skip (asked in sequence with PILLOK/SPANKING). Wording identical to MARHOMO; series substantively continuous. MARSAMEY is a 2022/2024 wording experiment variant ('same-sex couple' replacing 'homosexual couple'). To include in persistence analysis: harmonize MARHOMO (2016/2018) with MARSAME (2022/2024) and handle skip pattern (same concern as HAPMAR/HAPCOHAB).
Wording experiment variant of MARSAME: replaces 'homosexual couple' with 'same-sex couple'. Form 2 only. Parent variable MARSAME is the analysis item.
Wording experiment variant of XMOVIE: replaces 'X-rated movie' with 'pornography' and includes internet as a source. Form 2 only in 2022/2024. Parent variable XMOVIE is the analysis item.
